explanation: The LTL formula captures the requirement that whenever the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state and the sensor data is not good, in the next state the autopilot will be in the MANEUVER state. The chosen template is a standard reactive property where the antecedent expresses the condition for the transition and the consequent expresses the resulting state. The boolean expression for the antecedent is a conjunction of the current state being NOMINAL and the sensor data being not good, while the consequent is the next state being MANEUVER.

This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the system is in the NOMINAL state and the sensor data is not good, then in the next state, the system will be in the MANEUVER state.  This is expressed using the implication operator: "G (state_is_NOMINAL & !good) -> (X state_is_MANEUVER)", which states that globally (at all times), if the system is in the NOMINAL state and the sensor data is not good, then in the next state, the system will be in the MANEUVER state.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state and the sensor data is not good, in the next state the autopilot will be in the MANEUVER state. The bool_exp2 represents the autopilot being in the NOMINAL state, bool_exp3 represents the sensor data being not good, and X bool_exp3 represents the next state being the MANEUVER state.

The LTL formula captures the requirement that whenever the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state and the sensor data is not good (i.e., 'good' is false), the autopilot must transition to the MANEUVER state in the next time step. This is ensured by the implication "(state_is_NOMINAL & ! good) -> X state_is_MANEUVER" within the G operator, which guarantees the implication holds at all times.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state and the sensor data is not good (i.e., 'good' is false), then in the next time step, the autopilot will transition to the MANEUVER state (i.e., 'pullup' becomes true).

The property states that whenever the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state, and the sensor data is not good, the autopilot will eventually transition to the MANEUVER state. It captures the requirement by ensuring that the autopilot changes states from NOMINAL to MANEUVER upon detection of faulty sensor data.

This LTL formula asserts that whenever the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state (bool_exp2) and the sensor data is not good (!good), then in the next state (X), the autopilot will transition to the MANEUVER state (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement that the autopilot should change states from NOMINAL to MANEUVER when the sensor data is not good.

This LTL formula states that whenever the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state (bool_exp2) and the sensor data is not good (i.e., !good), then the next state will be MANEUVER (i.e., X state_is_MANEUVER). This captures the requirement that the autopilot should transition from NOMINAL to MANEUVER when sensor data is not good.

This LTL property ensures that if the system is in the NOMINAL mode and the sensor data is not good (indicated by !good), then in the next state the system will transition to the MANEUVER mode (indicated by pullup). The G operator ensures this property holds globally, meaning it holds at all times during the system's execution.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state (bool_exp2: state_is_NOMINAL) and the sensor data is not good (bool_exp1: !good), it must transition to the MANEUVER state (bool_exp3: state_is_MANEUVER) in the next time step.  The use of the next time step operator X reflects that the autopilot will immediately transition to the MANEUVER state upon detection of a fault, not after some time delay.

The boolean expression for bool_exp3 is "state_is_MANEUVER", which means the autopilot is in the MANEUVER state.

The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state, while bool_exp3 represents the condition that the sensor data is not good (i.e., the 'good' atomic proposition is negated).
